channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: UCLA set to break WNBA draft record with multiple first-round picks following NCAA title win
item.description: UCLA’s women’s basketball team is on the verge of setting a new WNBA record after its dominant run to the NCAA championship. As many as five Bruins players could be selected in the first round, with s...

item.title: Eight rescued from floodwaters in Siha District, Kilimanjaro
item.description: The Tanzania Fire and Rescue Force in the Kilimanjaro Region successfully rescued eight individuals in Koboko South Village, Nasai Ward, after their homes were surrounded by floodwaters caused by heav...

item.title: How Global Coffee Partnerships Support Tanzanian Smallholder Farmers
item.description: For more than 25 years, International Coffee Partners (ICP) has been working in Tanzania to strengthen the coffee sector through long-term collaboration with smallholder farmers. The initiative brings...

item.title: Chery plans to increase European car production through partnerships
item.description: Chinese automaker Chery is planning to expand its car production footprint in Europe by collaborating with established carmakers, leveraging existing factories instead of building new plants. Speaking...

item.title: Apple explores multiple prototype designs for upcoming smart glasses
item.description: Apple is reportedly advancing its work on smart glasses by testing four distinct design prototypes, according to details reported by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman and covered in TechCrunch. The company is s...

item.title: New Digital Module to Improve Procurement Transparency and Efficiency
item.description: Dodoma — The Public Procurement Appeals Authority (PPAA) in Tanzania has introduced a new digital module under the NeST system that is expected to greatly improve accountability, transparency and effi...

item.title: Geo-tagging Nigeria's POS Terminals: Balancing Security with Agent Livelihoods
item.description: This article yarns about the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) new move wey dem announce on August 25, 2025—mandatory geo-tagging of Point-of-Sale (POS) terminals. The aim be to make transactions more tra...

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Art, History, and Space Exploration
item.description: The latest edition of 'Fog Signals' from Moss & Fog covers the most compelling stories from April 6 to April 12, 2026, offering a diverse look at art, history, and science. The issue features a fascin...

item.title: Hoërskool Vryburg mourns three pupils killed in road accident involving school transport
item.description: Hoërskool Vryburg has announced the death of a third learner following a tragic road accident that took place last Wednesday. The crash involved four learners travelling from Delareyville to school at...

item.title: Cape Town Courts Deal with Surge in Gang-Related Criminal Cases
item.description: Cape Town courts are bracing for a significant caseload this week as investigations into recent gang-related violence in the Western Cape escalate. Following a shooting incident in Valhalla Park, Bish...

item.title: Toronto man suffers burns in Seaton House fire
item.description: A man suffered serious burns in a fire at Seaton House, a well-known shelter in Toronto, on April 12, 2026. Emergency services responded promptly and transported the man to a local hospital, where he ...

item.title: Police Escort Attacked During Polio Vaccination Campaign in Hangu
item.description: On Monday, in Hangu district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, a police constable lost his life and four other officers sustained injuries when a police team escorting a polio vaccination squad came under attack...

item.title: Kenya Explores Free Trade Agreement With India to Address Trade Imbalance
item.description: Kenya is weighing the possibility of signing a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) with India as part of its ongoing efforts to strengthen exports and reduce the large trade gap between the two countries. Prim...

item.title: Tanzanians Urged to Harvest and Store Rainwater Wisely
item.description: The article from Tanzania Daily News stresses the importance of harvesting rainwater as the rainy season continues across Tanzania. It reminds people that rainwater is a precious and natural resource ...

item.title: Delays at Milan Airport Leave British Travellers Stranded Due to New EU Border Rules
item.description: Dozens of British passengers found themselves stranded at Milan’s Linate Airport on Sunday following severe delays at border control linked to the European Union’s newly implemented Entry/Exit System ...

item.title: Faf de Klerk’s Rugby Journey: From Nelspruit to Bloemfontein via Three Continents
item.description: Francois Faf de Klerk’s rugby career has spanned three continents, reflecting a journey shaped as much by place as by performance. His professional debut came with the Pumas in Nelspruit, where the hi...

item.title: Understanding Boundaries in Digital Relationships
item.description: These days, social media makes people feel like they know influencers personally, even though the relationship is one-sided. Watching daily vlogs, 'get ready with me' videos, or casual updates creates...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Urges Protection for Lebanese Civilians Amid Rising Middle East Conflict
item.description: Pope Leo XIV has expressed deep concern for Lebanon, declaring there is a moral duty to safeguard its civilians amid the ongoing Israeli strikes that have reportedly killed over 2,000 people in the pa...

item.title: PM Modi urges prompt implementation of women's reservation law
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has emphasised the importance of addressing the long-pending demand for a women's reservation law in India. Speaking on the issue, he stated that decades of waiting for th...

item.title: Artemis II Marks Humanity’s Return to the Moon After More Than Half a Century
item.description: The Globe and Mail article by Ivan Semeniuk highlights the recent completion of NASA’s Artemis II mission, which reestablishes humanity’s ability to travel to the Moon after more than five decades. Th...
